channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Gene therapy is transforming lives, but for many Americans it's hard to reach
item.description: Gene therapies have the potential to cure some diseases, but they are extraordinarily expensive. Location can also be a big hurdle for patients seekin...

item.title: Elevated blood pressure at discharge from delivery hospitalization predicts earlier admission postpartum
item.description: 1. Blood pressure â¥140/90 mmHg at discharge predicts earlier postpartum admission but does not predict severity of hypertensive disease at readmission...

item.title: China and the US alter foreign aid strategies
item.description: China's foreign aid strategy has shifted in the last few decades and now its model may be the one the US is adopting as China moves away from it.

item.title: 'Fear of Flying Clinic' helps anxious travelers back into the skies
item.description: For 50 years, a San Francisco-based group has created a space where fearful flyers can get supported exposure to air travel.

item.title: Lab-grown reservoir cells aim at HIV's last strongholds
item.description: A new study has overcome a long-standing challenge: how to isolate and study elusive HIV-infected cells called authentic reservoir clones (ARCs) that ...

item.title: Could a gene lower nicotine dependence? What a CHRNB3 variant suggests
item.description: Variants in a nicotine receptor gene are associated with a lower likelihood of heavy smoking, according to a study published in Nature Communications....

item.title: Modern technology must revolutionize the archaic physician job search [PODCAST]
item.description: Subscribe to The Podcast by KevinMD. Watch on YouTube. Catch up on old episodes! Practicing anesthesiologist and the physician co-founder of Marit Hea...

item.title: What to Know About Chronic Cough in Patients Taking GLP-1s
item.description: Rates of the side effect appear to be increasing, but itâs not yet clear why. Medscape Medical News

item.title: Brief Program Improves QoL in Patients With Severe Allergies
item.description: Small gains in self-efficacy and injectable epinephrine prescription use were measured. Medscape Medical News

item.title: Kenya-Uganda trial reduces HIV incidence by 70% in rural populations
item.description: By pairing digital tools with tailored HIV services delivered by community health workers and clinicians, a study has reduced new HIV cases by 70% in ...

item.title: Ultrasound gives the brain a nudge in the right direction
item.description: Neuroscientist Soha Farboud of the Donders Institute at Radboud University has succeeded in adjusting activity in specific brain areas using a new tec...

item.title: NHS to Fund Ruxolitinib for Vitiligo
item.description: NICE has recommended topical ruxolitinib for facial non-segmental vitiligo in patients aged 12 or over after a revised cost review. Medscape News UK

item.title: Why maintenance of certification varies widely: a system in crisis
item.description: If you asked ten physicians to explain how they maintain board certification, youâd get at least ten different answers, maybe more. Some take quarterl...

item.title: A human mini-bladder shows the culprit of recurrent infections
item.description: Researchers at EPFL, Heidelberg University and Roche have built a human mini-bladder to show how urine composition weakens bladder tissue, helping inf...

item.title: Rise of NPs Leads to More Advanced Certifications
item.description: Much of the growth among the nurse practitioner workforce is expected to be in emergency medicine and more complex clinical roles. Medscape Medical Ne...

item.title: Fewer Newborns Receive Hepatitis B Vaccination
item.description: New data show newborn hepatitis B vaccination rates have dropped, raising concern over rising misinformation. Medscape Medical News

item.title: 2 Minute Medicine Rewind February 23, 2026
item.description: ADHD and adherence to antihypertensive medication treatment: a multinational cohort study 1. Adults with attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder are ...

item.title: Prenatal depression and gestational diabetes are associated with increased risk of childhood obesity
item.description: 1. Prenatal depression and gestational diabetes were independently associated with a higher risk of childhood obesity within the first 10 years of lif...

item.title: The FDA creates a quicker path for gene therapies
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration aims to evaluate treatments for rare diseases based on plausible evidence that they would work â without requiring a ...

item.title: BAFTAs apologize after guest with Tourette syndrome uses racial slur during ceremony
item.description: A man with Tourette syndrome shouted a racial slur and other offensive remarks during the BAFTA awards ceremony Sunday. The BBC did not edit out his o...

item.title: Physicians slightly more likely to die at home or hospice
item.description: 1. In this cross-sectional study, physicians were slightly more likely to die at home or in hospice than both the general population and individuals i...

item.title: Managing celiac disease: Overcoming the hidden social burden
item.description: After I got diagnosed with celiac disease when I was 19, I faced a difficult choice every time friends invited me out: I could either stay home, or I ...

item.title: AI governance in health care: Why physicians must lead the design
item.description: Artificial intelligence is no longer approaching medicine. It is already embedded inside it. It drafts our notes. It summarizes research. It suggests ...

item.title: Why death certificates fail to capture the reality of aging
item.description: What do old people die from? Ella passed away last week at age 84. I have cared for her since she was admitted to the nursing home two years ago. Her ...

item.title: Poly-L-lysine-coated catheters are safe and well-tolerated in adults requiring short-term catheterization
item.description: 1. Poly-L-lysine (PLL)-coated catheters are well-tolerated, safe, and noninferior in preventing bacteriuria compared to noble metal alloys (NMA)-coate...
